title: Gujarati Dal

overview.description: A sweet, tangy and gently spiced lentil curry made with toor dal, tomatoes and aromatic tempering. A classic everyday dish from Gujarat.

ingredients: 1 tbsp grated jaggery (or soft brown sugar)
method: Pressure cook with the water and turmeric for 15 minutes, then allow the pressure to release naturally.
Mash the cooked dal until smooth using a whisk or potato masher.
Heat the ghee over a medium heat.
Add the mustard seeds and allow them to pop.
Add the cumin seeds, dried chillies, curry leaves and asafoetida.
Add the onion and cook for 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in the ginger and green chillies and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes and cook until softened.
Stir in the ground coriander and ground cumin.
Stir in the jaggery and tamarind paste.
Simmer gently for 10–15 minutes until slightly thickened.

learning.did-you-know: Gujarati cuisine is famous for balancing sweet, sour and savoury flavours within the same dish, and Gujarati Dal is one of its best-known examples.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook toor dal, prepare a traditional tempering (tadka) and balance sweetness with acidity.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A completely different style of dal that shows the incredible regional variety of Indian cooking while using pantry staples.
chapman-tips: Pressure cooking produces the smoothest dal and saves considerable time.
The finished dal should taste balanced—sweetness, tanginess and savouriness should all be noticeable without any one dominating.
Fresh curry leaves make a significant difference if you have them frozen.
Roasted cumin powder can be sprinkled over the finished dal for extra aroma.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of water if the dal thickens in the fridge.
